meta_title: For Freelancers: email automation to Solve no version control (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to email automation and overcome no version control. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Email Automation to Solve No Version Control
content.seo_description: Learn how to use email automation to overcome version control gaps. A professional, intermediate case study using Antigravity and Claude Code for freelancers.
content.answer_block: When your project lacks version control, you face catastrophic data loss. By implementing automated email snapshots of your codebase, you create an immutable, timestamped audit trail. Using Antigravity to structure the logic and Claude Code for execution, you gain a safety net that protects your work and your professional reputation.
content.intro:
You’re three hours into a high-stakes client build, the coffee is cold, and you realize you just nuked the main production file. The sinking feeling isn't just about the code—it's about the client's trust. Without proper version control, your workflow is a house of cards waiting for a single gust of wind.

But what if you could weaponize your communication tools to act as a decentralized, automated backup system? By integrating email automation into your development lifecycle, you turn every major milestone into a persistent, searchable archive, ensuring that even if your local environment fails, your progress remains untouchable.

steps.title: Define the Backup Scope
steps.description: Identify the critical project directories that require snapshots. We focus on configuration files and core logic modules.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Python script that scans the current directory for modified files in the last 24 hours, excluding node_modules and hidden folders, and prepares them for archival.

steps.title: Configure SMTP Transport
steps.description: Establish a secure connection to your SMTP provider to handle the outbound delivery of your code snapshots.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a modular SMTP handler using secure environment variables that attaches a zip file to an email and sends it to a dedicated 'backups' email address.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add-dependency smtplib && claude configure --env SMTP_SERVER,SMTP_USER,SMTP_PASS

steps.title: Automate the Cron Job
steps.description: Schedule the execution of the backup script to run at the end of every productive session, ensuring zero manual overhead.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a system-level cron configuration that triggers the backup script every evening at 18:00 local time.

steps.title: Implement Version Metadata
steps.description: Ensure every email contains clear metadata including timestamp, branch name, and a summary of changes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Modify the backup script to prepend a metadata JSON file to the zip archive, detailing the current Git-less state of the project.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file backup_script.py --prompt 'Add a function to generate a metadata.json file before zipping.'

steps.title: Verification and Testing
steps.description: Run a dry-run test to ensure the email delivery pipeline is functional and the file integrity is maintained.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a test suite that simulates a failure event and verifies the script triggers the email notification successfully.

pro_tips: Use a dedicated 'archive' email alias (e.g., project-backups@domain.com) so these emails don't clutter your main inbox.
Compress your code into .tar.gz format to ensure the file maintains Linux/Unix file permissions across different systems.
Add a 'hash' verification step where the script calculates an MD5 sum of the zip before sending to verify integrity upon retrieval.
If your project is large, configure the script to send incremental diffs rather than full snapshots to keep your email attachments small.
Always store your SMTP credentials in a local .env file that is ignored by your project's primary file structure.
content.next_steps: Once your email automation is live, set up a simple 'Restore' script that can take an email attachment and automatically extract it back into your working directory.
content.conversion_block: Stop relying on luck and start building with professional-grade reliability. You have the tools; now apply the rigor. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
